title: Vincetoxicum angulatum
taxonomy: Vincetoxicum angulatum is a species of flowering plant in the dogbane family (Apocynaceae), which is a family of over 400 genera and 5,000 species. The Apocynaceae family is part of the order Gentianales, which includes other flowering plant families such as the gentian and loganberry families. Within the Gentianales order, the Apocynaceae family is further divided into subfamilies and tribes. This classification helps to organize and understand the relationships between different plant species. Vincetoxicum angulatum's classification within its family and order provides insight into its evolutionary history and characteristics.
uses: Vincetoxicum angulatum is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. Its roots and leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The plant is also used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In addition, it is used to boost the immune system and to reduce stress and anxiety. Furthermore, it is also used in the treatment of various gastrointestinal disorders.

constituents: Vincetoxicum angulatum contains several active constituents, including vincetoxicine, a diterpenoid alkaloid, and other compounds such as vincetoxicine-2 and angulatin. These constituents are known for their potential bioactivity and have been studied for their medicinal properties. The exact composition and proportions of these constituents may vary depending on factors such as the plant's origin and growing conditions. side_effects: Vincetoxicum angulatum is known to cause side effects, including gastrointestinal issues and allergic reactions. The plant contains toxic compounds that can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea in some individuals. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also lead to more severe side effects, such as liver damage and respiratory issues. In addition, the plant may interact with certain medications, exacerbating their side effects. It's essential to exercise caution when handling Vincetoxicum angulatum, as its toxicity can be unpredictable.
intro: Vincetoxicum angulatum, also known as the Narrow-leaved Vincetoxicum, is a perennial plant belonging to the Apocynaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Vincetoxicum angulatum include vincamine alkaloids, which have been found to have a positive impact on cardiovascular health and cognitive function. In traditional herbal medicine, Vincetoxicum angulatum is commonly prepared as a tincture or infused tea, often used to promote cardiovascular health and improve memory.
